tool.name: uONT assemble
tool.description: assemble ONT reads into polished contigs
tool.command: mkdir -p uont_workdir && uONT workflow assemble --input '$input_reads' --assembler-tool '$assembler_tool' --threads '$threads' --min-read-depth '$min_read_depth' --max-contigs '$max_contigs' --output-dir 'uont_workdir' #if str($debug) == "true": --debug #end if > '$run_log' 2>&1 && tar -cf '$workflow_archive' uont_workdir

param.name: min_read_depth
param.label: Minimum read depth
param.help: Used by the autocycler assembly path.

param.name: max_contigs
param.label: Maximum contigs
param.help: Used by the autocycler assembly path.

tool.help:
This tool wraps `uONT workflow assemble`.
Inputs:
- One ONT FASTQ or FASTQ.GZ dataset - Choice of assembler (`autocycler` or `flye`) - Thread count - Optional autocycler tuning parameters
Outputs:
- `polished_assembly.fasta` - `downsampled.fastq.gz` - A combined run log - A tar archive of the full workflow directory
This wrapper depends on the `uont` conda package from the `jodyphelan` channel. The local Galaxy config has been updated to include that channel for dependency resolution.
